evals.prompt: Implement a function that validates email addresses.
evals.expected_output: Follow TDD RGR loop: RED — write failing test first: test('validates valid email', () => { expect(validateEmail('user@example.com')).toBe(true); }). Verify it fails. GREEN — write minimal implementation to pass. REFACTOR — clean up. Never write production code without a prior failing test.

evals.prompt: I already wrote the implementation. Now I'll add tests to reach 80% coverage.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No test-after — writing tests post-implementation defeats TDD. The implementation must be deleted and restarted using Red-Green-Refactor. TDD is the Iron Law: NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Tests written after implementation do not validate design — they only verify existing behavior.

evals.prompt: Write tests for the order service that covers the happy path and three error scenarios.
evals.expected_output: Apply AAA structure for each test: Arrange (set up Order, mock DB and payment), Act (call createOrder()), Assert (verify result — one logical assertion per test). Mock HTTP, Time, and external APIs. Never mock fast internal services (<200ms). Minimum 80% coverage. Use test descriptions that read as behavior specs.

should_not_trigger: Design the database schema for a blog platform
